page_content:
ARCH488 Selected Topics in Architectural Preservation (1-4 Credits) Selected Topics in Architectural Preservation. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.

AREC254 Charting a Sustainable Future: Integrating Economics and Policy for Climate Action (3 Credits) Explores climate change through an economic perspective, focusing on how economic principles can inform effective climate policies. Students will learn about the causes and impacts of a changing climate, evaluate various policy approaches, and develop critical thinking skills. By the end of the semester, they will understand economic tools for addressing climate change and the associated political, socioeconomic, and ethical complexities.

ARCH489 Independent Studies in Architectural Preservation (1-4 Credits) Proposed work must have a faculty sponsor and receive approval of the Architecture Program Curriculum Committee. Restriction: Permission of ARCH-Architecture Program. Repeatable to: 6 credits if content differs.
